etymology.description: semantic 至(至) (zhì, "to arrive") + phonetic 刀(刀) (dāo) -> to arrive, reach
etymology.interpretation: The character 到(dào, "to") combines the meaning of 至(至) (zhì, "to arrive"), indicating movement or arrival at a destination, with the phonetic component 刀(刀) (dāo) to provide the pronunciation. This construction intuitively conveys the action of reaching a point or destination.

examples.chinese: 我刚到学校(我剛到學校)。
他从北京到上海(他從北京到上海)。
examples.english: He went from Beijing to Shanghai.

examples.chinese: 我们到那里时(我們到那裡時)，天已经黑了(天已經黑了)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen dào nàlǐ shí, tiān yǐjīng hēi le.
examples.english: By the time we got there, it was already dark.
